_comment: OCI IAM Policy for PatronAI — apply in OCI Console → Identity → Policies
_usage: Create this policy in your OCI compartment to grant PatronAI the minimum required permissions
policy_statements: Allow group patronai-scanners to manage objects in compartment <your-compartment> where target.bucket.name='patronai-scan-*'
Allow group patronai-scanners to manage buckets in compartment <your-compartment> where target.bucket.name='patronai-scan-*'
Allow group patronai-scanners to use ons-topics in compartment <your-compartment>
Allow group patronai-scanners to manage ons-subscriptions in compartment <your-compartment>
Allow group patronai-scanners to read instances in compartment <your-compartment>
Allow group patronai-scanners to read vnics in compartment <your-compartment>
Allow group patronai-scanners to read vnic-attachments in compartment <your-compartment>
_notes: Replace <your-compartment> with your actual compartment name
Create group 'patronai-scanners' in OCI Console → Identity → Groups
Add your OCI user to this group
Customer Secret Keys (S3-compat) are used for Object Storage — no extra policy needed
VCN Flow Logs require Logging service permissions — add separately if needed
